intro.kicker: Topic 7 of 28 • Chapter: Waves and Sound • Unit: Waves and Sound • Physics
intro.description: The speed of sound in any elastic medium depends on the elasticity and density of the medium: v = √(E/ρ). This topic covers . Key principles include: Elasticity effects; Medium properties. In NEET Physics, Velocity of Longitudinal Wave (Sound Wave) is tested directly and as part of multi-concept problems. Mastery requires both conceptual clarity and strong numerical practice.

expectedQuestions.description: Velocity of Longitudinal Wave (Sound Wave) features in NEET Physics as a direct question or embedded in multi-step problems. Detailed study of Waves and Sound is recommended.

timeRequired.description: Theory 10–15 min, plus solving 15 practice problems. Efficient if you know the formula derivation.

difficulty.description: Waves and Sound difficulty level. Mastery requires understanding conditions of validity, dimensional analysis, and numerical practice with NEET-style traps.

cards.title: Core Concept: Velocity of Longitudinal Wave (Sound Wave)
cards.coreRevision: Velocity of Longitudinal Wave (Sound Wave) is a key pillar of Waves and Sound in NEET Physics. Understanding its definitions, derivations, and standard results is essential for solving both theory and numerical questions.

cards.title: NEET Traps to Avoid
cards.coreRevision: NEET setters target conceptual gaps in Velocity of Longitudinal Wave (Sound Wave). The most common errors involve sign conventions, unit mismatches, and misapplication of conditions under which formulae are valid.
points: Check sign conventions carefully (vector vs scalar quantities)
Verify that all conditions for the formula are satisfied
Dimensional analysis can catch formula errors instantly

curriculum.hintText: Topics in Velocity of Longitudinal Wave (Sound Wave) covered differently or not at all in AP Physics vs NEET.

gaps.title: Velocity of Longitudinal Wave (Sound Wave) in US vs Indian Curriculum
gaps.description: Wave motion and sound (Doppler, interference, organ pipes, beats) are covered in AP Physics but NEET includes standing wave resonance conditions in detail and Sonometer/Kundt's tube apparatus problems.
bullets: NEET expects derivation-level mastery; AP Physics often treats Velocity of Longitudinal Wave (Sound Wave) as applied only.
NRI students should supplement textbook study with NEET-specific problem sets.
Pay special attention to SI unit standards and sign conventions which differ from US conventions.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes Velocity of Longitudinal Wave (Sound Wave) in the context of NEET Physics?

options.text: Velocity of Longitudinal Wave (Sound Wave) applies only under idealized conditions with no friction or air resistance
Velocity of Longitudinal Wave (Sound Wave) is a fundamental result derivable from first principles and applies within well-defined physical conditions
Velocity of Longitudinal Wave (Sound Wave) has no mathematical formulation and is purely qualitative
Velocity of Longitudinal Wave (Sound Wave) contradicts Newton's laws in non-inertial frames

questions.explanation: Velocity of Longitudinal Wave (Sound Wave) is a rigorously derived physical principle within Waves and Sound. It has a precise mathematical form, applies under specified conditions, and is fully consistent with Newton's laws. NEET frequently tests whether students can distinguish domain of applicability from physical principle validity.

questions.question: A NEET student studying Velocity of Longitudinal Wave (Sound Wave) should prioritise which of the following?

options.text: Understanding the physical basis, deriving formulae, and solving numerical examples
Skipping this topic since it has low weightage
Using only dimensional analysis and avoiding formula application

questions.explanation: NEET rewards conceptual understanding combined with numerical accuracy. For Velocity of Longitudinal Wave (Sound Wave), students must understand the derivation, know the conditions of validity, and practice at least 15-20 NEET-style MCQs. Pure memorisation without understanding leads to errors on application-type questions.

questions.answer: This is the defining principle of Velocity of Longitudinal Wave (Sound Wave)

items.question: How many questions from Velocity of Longitudinal Wave (Sound Wave) appear in NEET each year?
items.answer: Velocity of Longitudinal Wave (Sound Wave) typically contributes 0–2 questions per NEET paper depending on the year. While it may not appear every year, it often features in multi-concept problems linked to adjacent topics in Waves and Sound. Skipping it is risky.

items.question: Is Velocity of Longitudinal Wave (Sound Wave) in the NEET 2025 syllabus?
items.answer: Yes, Velocity of Longitudinal Wave (Sound Wave) remains part of the NEET Physics syllabus under Waves and Sound. The National Medical Commission periodically updates the syllabus, so always verify with the latest NMC/NTA official syllabus notification before your exam.

items.question: What is the best way to revise Velocity of Longitudinal Wave (Sound Wave) before NEET?
items.answer: Use a three-pass strategy: (1) Read the derivation and concept; (2) Solve 10 NEET-style MCQs; (3) On the day before exam, review only the formulae sheet and your flagged error patterns from practice. Focus on conditions of validity and sign conventions.

items.question: What is the most common mistake in Velocity of Longitudinal Wave (Sound Wave) problems?
items.answer: The most frequent errors in Velocity of Longitudinal Wave (Sound Wave) are: applying the formula outside its domain (e.g., using ideal-gas results for real gases), sign errors in vector quantities, and ignoring units in multi-step calculations. Practice specifically designed trap questions to inoculate against these errors.

grounding_excerpt: (2) Newton's formula: He assumed that when sound propagates through air temperature remains constant. i.e. the process is isothermal. For isothermal process
(3) Laplace correction: He modified Newton's formula assuming that propagation of sound in gaseous medium is adiabatic process. For adiabatic process
(2) Effect of temperature: With rise in temperature velocity of sound increases.
(4) Effect of humidity: With increase in humidity, density of air decreases. So with rise in humidity velocity of sound increases.
